_meta.note: All content translated to English and structured from the source PDF. Placeholder fields (empty strings) are yours to fill: emails, URLs, thumbnails, photos, bio wording. Ministry (MEN) point values from the original were omitted as Poland-specific; venue rankings (CORE / journal quartile) were kept. Nothing here is styling — this is pure data so you can restyle freely later.

profile.name: Aleksander Plocharski
profile.title: PhD Candidate in Computer Graphics
affiliations: Warsaw University of Technology — Faculty of Mathematics and Information Science
Computer Graphics Research Group, Akces NCBR
profile.bio_draft: I work on the precision and controllability of neural models for graphics — neural signed distance fields, procedural and neuro-symbolic modeling, and machine learning for geometry. Before the PhD I was an R&D engineer at CD PROJEKT RED.

highlights.text: Three top-venue papers — EUROGRAPHICS ×2, Computer Graphics Forum

highlights.label: service
highlights.text: IPC member — EUROGRAPHICS 2026, Short Papers track

highlights.label: award
highlights.text: 2nd place — ACM SIGGRAPH 2024 Student Research Competition (Graduate)

notes.hobbies: Hobbies & interests outside research will live here — coming soon.

awards.title: 2nd Place — ACM SIGGRAPH 2024 Student Research Competition (Graduate Student category)
awards.work: Neuro-Symbolic Transformation of Architectural Facades into Their Procedural Representations
awards.note: Presented at SIGGRAPH 2024 and defended before the competition jury after the first selection round.
awards.jury_comment: Congratulations on winning Second Place in the ACM SIGGRAPH 2024 Student Research Competition for graduate work! Your submission Neuro-Symbolic Transformation of Architectural Facades into Their Procedural Representations (SIGGRAPH 2024 Poster) was impressive in its execution, expansion upon state-of-the-art ideas, practicality, and visual results. Your presentation was also very well received by the jury.

courses_created.course: Applications of Modern AI Methods in Computer Graphics
courses_created.description: Designed, coordinated and taught a new elective closely tied to my research. It gives students a smooth entry into applying AI in computer graphics through hands-on projects, so they can confidently continue on their own with modern methods and technologies.

experience.org: Faculty of Mathematics and Information Science, Warsaw University of Technology
experience.description: Research in computer graphics and geometric modeling with related publications; teaching, coordinating and creating courses.

Member of the Computer Graphics Research Group. Research on the precision and controllability of neural models and their generated results; discussions with companies on potential commercialization and preparing research code for it.
Same role as above; employer name changed following a merger, with no change to role or responsibilities.

Developed solutions for geometry processing and procedural generation (specifics under NDA); combined state-of-the-art methods from the literature with new solutions and implemented prototypes (C++, Unreal Engine, Python).
Taught laboratory classes at the Faculty of Mathematics and Information Science.
Lead Programmer on a battle-simulator game (core systems, task planning and distribution, code reviews); Mid-Level Programmer on a large 4X game (gameplay and data-management systems).
Built and maintained a web app for assessing foreign-language proficiency (PHP).
Developed a method for estimating a person's known vocabulary size in a foreign language (Python).
